title: Abstracts
generation_instructions: Provide a concise overview of the project, including the background, aims, methods, expected results, and importance to medicine. Include five keywords. Ensure the abstract is understandable to a scientifically literate reader. Do not include proprietary information. Adhere to the 30-line limit as specified by NIH guidelines. This section should clearly and succinctly describe the project's health-related relevance.
search_queries: elements of a good research abstract
how to write a lay summary of research

title: Research Plan
generation_instructions: Develop a detailed research plan including scientific background, research question, study design with a detailed plan, methods (sample size justification and available equipment), preliminary results, schematic representation with partner responsibilities and timeline, and time estimate for each stage. Include up to 15 references. Address data management and sharing plans, and authentication of key biological/chemical resources. Adhere to NIH guidelines for research rigor and reproducibility. Ensure all experimental details are cited and referenced appropriately. Address potential hazards and safety precautions. If human fetal tissue is involved, follow specific guidelines for justification and documentation.
search_queries: elements of a strong research proposal
NIH data management and sharing plan example
authentication of key biological resources NIH
human fetal tissue research NIH guidelines

title: English Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a one-page English abstract including background and aim, methods, expected results, importance to medicine, and five keywords. Ensure it is a succinct and accurate description of the proposed work.
search_queries: how to write a grant abstract

title: Hebrew Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a one-page Hebrew abstract including background and aim, methods, expected results, importance to medicine, and five keywords. Ensure it is a succinct and accurate description of the proposed work.
search_queries: how to write a grant abstract in Hebrew

title: Layman's Terms Abstract
generation_instructions: Write a one-page abstract in layman's terms, including background and aim, methods, expected results, importance to medicine, and five keywords. Ensure it is understandable to a general audience.
search_queries: how to write a plain language summary
writing for a general audience tips

title: Scientific Background
generation_instructions: Provide a comprehensive scientific background, including a review of relevant literature, identification of gaps in knowledge, and the rationale for the proposed research. Cite published experimental details and provide full references.
search_queries: how to write a compelling background

title: Research Question
generation_instructions: Clearly state the research question and hypothesis. Define the specific aims and objectives of the proposed research. Ensure the question is focused and testable.
search_queries: how to formulate a research question
writing specific aims for a grant

title: Study Design
generation_instructions: Describe the study design, including the research methods, data collection procedures, and analysis plan. Justify the chosen design and explain how it will address the research question. Address relevant biological variables, such as sex. If applicable, address inclusion/exclusion criteria for human subjects, and plans for inclusion of women and minorities.
search_queries: NIH inclusion of women and minorities guidelines
addressing sex as a biological variable in research

title: Methods
generation_instructions: Provide a detailed description of the methods, procedures, and techniques to be used in the research. Include sample size justification and a list of available equipment. Describe methods to ensure the identity and validity of key biological and/or chemical resources.
search_queries: authentication of key biological resources methods

title: Preliminary Results
generation_instructions: Present preliminary results and pilot data to demonstrate the feasibility of the proposed research. Discuss the PI's preliminary studies, data, and/or experience pertinent to this application.
search_queries: how to present preliminary data effectively

title: Time Estimate
generation_instructions: Provide a time estimate for each stage of the research, including a schematic representation with partner responsibilities and a timeline. Include key milestones and deliverables.
